title: Login with valid credentials
steps: Go to login.html. Enter valid email and password. Click Log In.
expected: Redirects to dashboard. Sidebar shows correct tenant name, role badge, and display name.

title: Login with wrong password
steps: Go to login.html. Enter valid email and a wrong password. Click Log In.
expected: Error message appears below the form. Page does not redirect.

title: Logout
steps: While logged in, click Log Out in the sidebar.
expected: Redirected to login.html. Navigating back to the app redirects to login.html.

title: Session persists across page refresh
steps: Log in. Press F5 or Ctrl+R to refresh the page.
expected: Dashboard reloads. User is still logged in. Sidebar still shows correct name and role.

title: Forgot password flow
steps: Go to login.html. Click 'Forgot password?'. Enter your email. Click Send.
expected: Success message appears. A password reset email is sent to the inbox.

title: KPIs load correctly
steps: Log in and view the Overview tab.
expected: Utilization %, bench count, and billable headcount KPI cards all show numeric values (not blank or error).

title: Bench list shows correct consultants
steps: On the Overview tab, view the Bench section.
expected: Consultants with 0–10h/week this week appear in the bench list with their name and current hours.

title: Rolloff list accuracy
steps: On the Overview tab, view the Upcoming Rolloffs section.
expected: Consultants whose project assignments end in the next 4 weeks appear, with project name and end date.

title: Demand cliffs display
steps: On the Overview tab, view the Demand Cliffs section.
expected: Weeks where demand drops significantly are highlighted. Each row shows the week and the drop in hours.

title: Needs coverage donut chart renders
steps: On the Overview tab, find the Open Needs donut chart.
expected: Donut chart renders with colored segments. Legend shows client names and counts.

title: Donut chart click filters needs list
steps: On the Overview tab, click a segment on the Open Needs donut chart.
expected: The needs list below the chart filters to show only needs for that client. Clicking again clears the filter.

title: Chart tooltips display data
steps: On the Overview tab, hover over bars or segments in any chart.
expected: Tooltip appears showing relevant data (week label, hours, consultant name, etc.).

title: Dashboard refreshes on Ctrl+R
steps: On the Overview tab, press Ctrl+R.
expected: Dashboard data reloads. KPIs and charts update. No full page refresh occurs.

title: Heatmap loads correct consultants for tenant
steps: Navigate to the Resource Allocation tab.
expected: Heatmap shows only consultants belonging to your tenant. No consultants from other tenants appear.

title: Color coding: bench, partial, utilized, overallocated
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, observe cell colors across the heatmap.
expected: Red cells = 0–10h (bench). Yellow = 11–44h (partial). Green = 45h (utilized). Orange = 46h+ (over).

title: Expand consultant row shows project breakdown
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, click the chevron/arrow next to a consultant name.
expected: Row expands to show per-project sub-rows with individual project hours per week.

title: Inline cell editing — click, type, blur to save
steps: Click a cell in the heatmap. Type a new number. Click elsewhere or press Tab.
expected: Cell saves the new value. Cell color updates to reflect new utilization level. No page reload.

title: Save confirmation appears after edit
steps: Edit a heatmap cell and save (blur or Tab).
expected: A brief save confirmation message appears (e.g. toast or status indicator), then disappears.

title: Keyboard nav: Tab moves between cells
steps: Click a heatmap cell. Press Tab repeatedly.
expected: Focus moves to the next cell in the row, then wraps to the next row. Each focused cell is editable.

title: Keyboard nav: Enter moves down
steps: Click a heatmap cell. Type a value. Press Enter.
expected: Value is saved and focus moves to the same column in the next row.

title: Quick Fill functionality
steps: Click Quick Fill for a consultant+project row. Fill in a value and confirm.
expected: The specified hours are written to the selected week range. Heatmap cells update.

title: Collapse and expand all rows
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, click Expand All, then Collapse All.
expected: Expand All opens all consultant sub-rows. Collapse All hides all sub-rows. Performance stays smooth.

title: Heatmap virtual scroll performance
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, scroll rapidly up and down through all consultants.
expected: Scrolling is smooth (no freezing or jank). Rows render correctly without blank gaps.

title: Create new staffing need
steps: On the Open Needs tab, click Add Need. Fill in level, skills, hours/week, start and end dates. Click Save.
expected: New need appears in the list with correct details. Donut chart updates.

title: Edit existing need
steps: On the Open Needs tab, click Edit on an existing need. Change one field. Click Save.
expected: Need updates with the new value. Change is reflected in the list immediately.

title: Close need as met
steps: On the Open Needs tab, click Close on a need. Select 'Met'. Confirm.
expected: Need disappears from the open needs list. Donut chart segment shrinks or disappears.

title: Close need as abandoned
steps: On the Open Needs tab, click Close on a need. Select 'Abandoned'. Confirm.
expected: Need disappears from the open needs list.

title: Candidate matching shows eligible consultants
steps: On the Open Needs tab, click Find Candidates for an open need.
expected: Modal shows consultants matching the required level and skills. Each candidate shows available hours and a match badge (green/yellow/coral).

title: Bulk assign consultants to need
steps: Open candidates for a need. Select one or more consultants. Click Assign.
expected: Consultants are assigned to the need's project for the need's date range. Heatmap updates.

title: Auto-close when need fully staffed
steps: Assign enough consultants to fully meet a need's required hours.
expected: Need automatically closes with reason 'met'. It disappears from the open needs list.

title: Urgency badges display correctly
steps: On the Open Needs tab, observe the urgency badges on needs.
expected: Needs starting ≤2 weeks out show 'Urgent' (red). 2–4 weeks show 'Soon' (yellow). 4+ weeks show 'Planned' (green).

title: Donut chart segments by client
steps: On the Open Needs tab, view the donut chart.
expected: Each segment represents one client. Segment size reflects the number of open needs for that client.

title: Donut chart click filters needs list
steps: Click a segment on the Open Needs donut chart.
expected: The needs list filters to show only that client's needs. Clicking the same segment again clears the filter.

title: Needs list sorted correctly
steps: On the Open Needs tab, view the full needs list.
expected: Needs are ordered by urgency (soonest start date first).

title: Skill matching uses full skill set
steps: Create a need requiring a secondary skill (not the consultant's primary skill). Open candidates.
expected: Consultants with that skill in their full skill set appear as candidates, even if it is not their primary skill.

title: Suggested questions load on tab open
steps: Click the Ask Claude tab.
expected: 3–5 suggested question chips appear within a few seconds of opening the tab.

title: Free-form question returns relevant answer
steps: On the Ask Claude tab, type 'Who is currently on the bench?' and press Enter or click Ask.
expected: Claude responds with a list of consultants with low utilization from your tenant's data.

title: Response references correct tenant data
steps: Ask Claude: 'List all consultants.' Review the response.
expected: Response contains only consultants from your tenant. No consultants from other tenants appear.

title: Follow-up question works
steps: Ask Claude a question. After the response, ask a follow-up related question.
expected: Follow-up question returns a relevant response. Both questions and answers are visible in the chat history.

title: Error state on empty question
steps: On the Ask Claude tab, click Ask without typing anything.
expected: An error or warning message appears. No API call is made.

title: Consultant list loads
steps: Navigate to Settings → Consultants.
expected: Full list of consultants for the tenant loads. Each row shows name, level, and status.

title: Edit consultant profile
steps: In Settings → Consultants, click a consultant. Edit their name or location. Click Save.
expected: Changes save and are reflected in the consultant list and heatmap immediately.

title: Add skill to consultant
steps: In Settings → Consultants, open a consultant profile. Add a new skill from the dropdown. Save.
expected: Skill appears in the consultant's skill list. Skill is used in candidate matching going forward.

title: Remove skill from consultant
steps: In Settings → Consultants, open a consultant profile. Remove an existing skill. Save.
expected: Skill is removed from the consultant's profile.

title: Deactivate consultant
steps: In Settings → Consultants, click Deactivate on an active consultant. Confirm.
expected: Consultant's status changes to Inactive. They no longer appear in the heatmap or candidate lists.

title: Reactivate consultant
steps: In Settings → Consultants, find an inactive consultant. Click Reactivate. Confirm.
expected: Consultant's status changes back to Active. They reappear in the heatmap.

title: User list loads
steps: Navigate to Settings → Users.
expected: All users for the tenant appear in the list with email, role, and status.

title: Invite new user — generates link, no password field
steps: In Settings → Users, click Invite User. Enter an email and role. Click Send Invite.
expected: An invite link is generated and shown. No password field is present. The user appears in the list as 'pending'.

title: Change user role
steps: In Settings → Users, click the role dropdown for a user. Select a different role. Confirm.
expected: User's role updates in the list. The user must log in again for the change to take effect.

title: Deactivate user
steps: In Settings → Users, click Deactivate on an active user. Confirm.
expected: User's status changes to Deactivated. They cannot log in.

title: Reactivate user
steps: In Settings → Users, find a deactivated user. Click Reactivate. Confirm.
expected: User's status changes back to Active. They can log in again.

title: Admin: can edit heatmap cells
steps: Log in as admin. Go to Resource Allocation. Click a cell and enter a number.
expected: Cell is editable and saves successfully.

title: Admin: can close and assign needs
steps: Log in as admin. Go to Open Needs. Close a need and assign a consultant to another.
expected: Both actions succeed without permission errors.

title: Admin: can access Settings: Consultants and Users
steps: Log in as admin. Go to Settings. Check that both Consultants and Users sub-panels are visible.
expected: Both Consultants and Users nav items appear in Settings sidebar.

title: Admin: can see billing rates in consultant profile
steps: Log in as admin. Open a consultant profile in Settings → Consultants.
expected: Billing rate field is visible and shows a numeric value.

title: Resource Manager: can edit heatmap and assign needs
steps: Log in as resource manager. Edit a heatmap cell. Then assign a consultant to a need.
expected: Both actions succeed. RM has full data-edit access.

title: Resource Manager: can see Consultants but not Users in Settings
steps: Log in as resource manager. Go to Settings.
expected: Consultants panel is visible and functional. Users panel is not shown.

title: Resource Manager: can see billing rates
steps: Log in as resource manager. Open a consultant profile.
expected: Billing rate is visible.

title: Project Manager: heatmap is read-only
steps: Log in as project manager. Go to Resource Allocation. Try to click and edit a cell.
expected: Cells are not editable. No input field appears on click.

title: Project Manager: can create and edit needs but not close or assign
steps: Log in as project manager. Create a new need. Edit it. Then try to close it or assign a consultant.
expected: Create and edit succeed. Close and Assign buttons are either hidden or return a permission error.

title: Project Manager: cannot see billing rates
steps: Log in as project manager. Open a consultant profile.
expected: Billing rate field is hidden or not shown.

title: Executive: read-only dashboard and heatmap
steps: Log in as executive. View the Overview and Resource Allocation tabs.
expected: All data loads correctly. No edit controls appear. Heatmap cells are not clickable.

title: Executive: can access Ask Claude
steps: Log in as executive. Click the Ask Claude tab. Type a question.
expected: Ask Claude tab is visible. Question returns a valid response.

title: Executive: cannot access Settings
steps: Log in as executive. Look for Settings in the sidebar.
expected: Settings tab is not visible in the sidebar.

title: Consultant: sees only own heatmap row
steps: Log in as a consultant-role user linked to a consultant record.
expected: Resource Allocation heatmap shows only that consultant's own row. No other consultants visible.

title: Consultant: no billing rates visible
steps: Log in as consultant. Open your own profile.
expected: Billing rate field is not visible.

title: Dashboard shows only tenant's data
steps: Log in as admin of one tenant. View KPIs, bench list, and rolloffs on the Overview tab.
expected: All data belongs to your tenant only. Log in to a different tenant and confirm the numbers differ.

title: Heatmap shows only tenant's employees
steps: Log in as admin of one tenant. View Resource Allocation heatmap. Note the consultant names.
expected: Only consultants from your tenant appear. Log in to a different tenant and verify different consultants appear.

title: Ask Claude references only tenant's data
steps: Log in as admin of one tenant. Ask Claude: 'List all consultants.' Note the names. Log in as admin of another tenant and ask the same question.
expected: Each tenant sees only their own consultants. No cross-tenant data leaks.

title: Admin can reset sandbox
steps: Log in as admin on a non-production tenant (Acme Corp, BigCo Inc, or Summit LLC). Go to Settings → Consultants or another data area. Trigger a sandbox reset if available.
expected: Reset completes successfully. Synthetic data is restored. Dashboard data reloads.

title: Reset rejected for production tenant
steps: Log in as admin on the Meridian Consulting (production) tenant. Attempt to trigger a sandbox reset.
expected: Reset is rejected with an error. No data is modified.
